summary: Route Rover personalized travel planner app with an interactive/realistic homepage UI, weather forecasting/alerts, popular destinations, transportation suggestions, and map-based distance tracking (with optional non-LLM chat UI).
architectural_notes: Platform limitation: external LLM integrations (e.g., ChatGPT/OpenAI) are not supported; only a standalone chat UI can be added.
Weather alerts/forecast intended via public weather API calls from the frontend.

existing_features.title: Revamp the Home page (/), replacing the current hero/background experience with a more interactive and realistic travel-themed hero (e.g., animated route lines, subtle depth/parallax, responsive motion), while keeping the existing login/navigation behavior (auto-redirect logged-in users to /dashboard).

Implement real weather forecasting and weather alerts for selected destinations by replacing the current mocked weather data in the WeatherWidget with data fetched from a public weather API that does not require an API key (or uses a documented free endpoint), and surface alerts prominently in the UI.

Add a “Popular Destinations” experience across the app: expand the destination set and present it as an engaging, browsable section (cards/carousel/grid) with filtering/sorting, and connect selection to the existing destination-driven widgets (weather, transport, traffic).

Enhance transportation suggestions so users can compare realistic options for a selected destination, including at minimum duration, estimated cost, and a simple recommendation indicator (e.g., fastest/cheapest/eco), and allow users to set an origin (city or coordinates) to make results contextual.
Add an in-app map view that lets users place/select an origin and destination and shows the distance between them in kilometers, with clear UI controls and readable distance output.

Add an in-app chat assistant UI (no ChatGPT/OpenAI integration) that provides local, rule-based/help-content responses related to the app’s travel planning features (weather, destinations, budget, transport, distance).

Unify the visual theme across Home, Login, Dashboard, and destination-related components to match the Route Rover logo’s warm palette (amber/orange/rose) and reduce conflicting blue/purple gradients, while keeping dark mode support.

new_feature_requests.summary: Revamp homepage to be more interactive and realistic (replace current interactive homepage/background styling)
Add weather forecasting and weather alerts
Add map feature and track destination distance in kilometers
Add in-app chat assistant UI (no ChatGPT/OpenAI integration)
